description: Canonical maturity assessment rubric. All agents use this to evaluate repo maturity on the same scale.

assessment_protocol.branch_rule: Always assess the branch/worktree you are currently on. Record branch name and HEAD SHA. Cross-agent comparison REQUIRES both agents on the same branch.
assessment_protocol.execution_rule: MUST run evidence_commands listed per area. Read-only file inspection alone is insufficient for scoring. All evidence_commands output must be recorded.
assessment_protocol.scoring_rule: Score based on criteria definitions. L0=0%, L1=25%, L2=50%, L3=75%, L4=100%. Band interpolation: 0 gaps to next level = band top (e.g. L2=62%), 1-2 gaps = band mid (50%), 3+ gaps = band bottom (38%). For L3 and L4: ALL conditions listed must be met; partial match = previous level.
assessment_protocol.conflict_resolution: If two agents score differently, the one that ran more evidence_commands wins. Attach command output as evidence.
assessment_protocol.self_reference_rule: The maturity rubric policy itself (policy_maturity_assessment.v1.json) must NOT be counted as evidence for any area score. Only operational policies count.
assessment_protocol.l3_strict_rule: L3 criteria use absolute quantifiers (all, every, always). If ANY condition is not met, score as L2 with interpolation. Do not round up to L3 for partial compliance.
assessment_protocol.evidence_threshold_rule: Evidence commands that produce counts (e.g. file counts, match counts) must be compared against explicit thresholds defined in criteria. High count alone does not imply high maturity.

criteria.l1_definition: State machines defined with states/transitions; schema exists; no runtime code
criteria.l2_definition: State store exists (work_item_state.py); trajectory tests pass; but no runtime transition guard
criteria.l3_definition: validate_transition() called before every state write; invalid transitions BLOCKED; CI contract test
criteria.l4_definition: L3 + transition metrics (count, latency, rejection rate) exported to observability

criteria.l0_definition: No atomic write pattern; direct open().write() everywhere
criteria.l1_definition: Atomic write helper exists (tmp+rename) but not consistently used
criteria.l2_definition: Atomic write + fsync on critical paths; but scattered direct writes remain; no WAL
criteria.l3_definition: All stateful writes go through durable_write; SQLite WAL+synchronous=NORMAL; CI gate blocks direct writes
criteria.l4_definition: L3 + crash recovery on startup; write durability matrix documented; CAS/revision on mutable stores

criteria.l0_definition: No decision schema or boundary logic
criteria.l1_definition: Schemas defined (decision-quality, risk-scoring, human-approval); no runtime wiring
criteria.l2_definition: Runtime code exists (decision_boundary.py, quality_gate.py) but not called from runner pipeline
criteria.l3_definition: enforce_decision_boundary() in runner pre-exec; quality gates enforce reject/suspend; approval TTL timeout live
criteria.l4_definition: L3 + decision quality trend alerts; provider benchmark comparison; human approval SLA metrics

evidence_commands: python3 -c "import json,glob; files=[f for f in glob.glob('policies/*.json') if 'maturity' not in f and 'fail_action' in open(f).read()]; print(f'operational policies with fail_action: {len(files)}'); [print(f'  {f}') for f in files]"

criteria.l1_definition: Schema defined (AND/OR/NOT combinators, fail_action); no runtime evaluator
criteria.l2_definition: Build-time composition exists (work-intake fragments); but no generic runtime engine; fail_action unused
criteria.l3_definition: Generic RuleEvaluator with AND/OR/NOT; fail_action enforced in policies; conflict detection; CI test
criteria.l4_definition: L3 + dynamic rule loading; rule evaluation metrics; hot-reload without restart

evidence_commands: for f in ci/*contract*.py ci/*test*.py; do basename $f; done | while read t; do grep -rl "$t" .github/workflows/ 2>/dev/null | wc -l | xargs -I{} echo "$t -> {} workflow refs"; done 2>/dev/null | head -20

criteria.l1_definition: Basic schema validation gate; manual runs
criteria.l2_definition: Multiple gates (schema, policy, enforcement); but <80% of contract tests wired to merge gate workflows
criteria.l3_definition: >=80% of ci/*contract* and ci/*test* files referenced in workflow YAMLs; merge blocked on failure; coverage report generated. ALL conditions required.
criteria.l4_definition: L3 + gate duration metrics; flaky test detection; auto-retry with evidence; gate-level SLA

criteria.l1_definition: Policies exist and validate against schemas; but no runtime loader validation
criteria.l2_definition: Schema coverage good; dry-run works; but fail_action not used in operational policies; runtime loaders skip validation
criteria.l3_definition: ALL of: (a) load_policy_validated() helper exists and used by loaders, (b) fail_action on >50% operational policies, (c) dry-run in CI workflow. ALL conditions required.
criteria.l4_definition: L3 + policy drift detection; deprecation lifecycle; policy effectiveness metrics

evidence_commands: for d in extensions/PRJ-*/; do echo "$d: manifest=$(test -f ${d}extension.manifest.v1.json && echo YES || echo NO)"; done
for d in extensions/PRJ-*/; do tests=$(find $d -name '*test*' -o -name '*contract*' 2>/dev/null | wc -l); echo "$(basename $d): $tests test files"; done

criteria.l1_definition: Extension directories exist with manifests; but minimal tests
criteria.l2_definition: All manifests present; docs_ref and ai_context_refs present; but not all extensions have >=2 test files
criteria.l3_definition: ALL extensions have: manifest + >=2 test files (smoke + contract). Manifest schema validation passes. ALL conditions required.
criteria.l4_definition: L3 + extension health dashboard exists (code evidence); auto-disable on contract failure implemented; version compatibility matrix generated. Must have code evidence, not just docs.

criteria.l1_definition: AGENTS.md exists as shared SSOT; multiple agent configs present
criteria.l2_definition: Shared ops commands; context bootstrap defined; but instruction drift between agents (e.g. .gemini/INSTRUCTIONS.md contains stale refs like src.ops.main)
criteria.l3_definition: ALL of: (a) context_drift_contract_test passes, (b) .gemini/INSTRUCTIONS.md has zero stale refs (src.ops.main=0), (c) bootstrap code shared. ALL conditions required.
criteria.l4_definition: L3 + cross-agent evaluation consistency (maturity rubric); shared context health dashboard

criteria.l1_definition: Print/basic logging scattered; trace_meta concept exists but trace file count alone is not sufficient
criteria.l2_definition: Custom trace_meta attached to summaries; decision quality JSONL; but structured logger count=0 OR OTEL exporter count=0
criteria.l3_definition: ALL of: (a) import logging/getLogger count > 0, (b) OTEL exporter code exists, (c) metrics Counter/Histogram in decision or gate code. ALL conditions required — trace_meta file count alone is NOT sufficient for L3.
criteria.l4_definition: L3 + dashboard code/config exists; alerting on degradation implemented; trace correlation across runner/gate/policy. Must have code evidence.

evidence_commands: python3 -c "import ast,sys; t=ast.parse(open('src/ops/manage.py').read()); cmds=[n.s for n in ast.walk(t) if isinstance(n,ast.Constant) and isinstance(n.s,str) and '-' in n.s and len(n.s)<40]; print(f'ops commands: {len(cmds)}')"

python3 -c "import ast; t=ast.parse(open('src/ops/manage.py').read()); cmds={n.s for n in ast.walk(t) if isinstance(n,ast.Constant) and isinstance(n.s,str) and '-' in n.s and len(n.s)<40}; import glob; tests={f.split('test_')[-1].replace('.py','').replace('_','-') for f in glob.glob('tests/**/*test*.py',recursive=True)+glob.glob('ci/*contract*.py')+glob.glob('ci/*test*.py')}; covered=cmds&tests; print(f'commands:{len(cmds)} tests:{len(tests)} covered:{len(covered)} ratio:{len(covered)/max(len(cmds),1):.0%}')"

criteria.l1_definition: Some contract tests exist; but no coverage mapping to ops commands
criteria.l2_definition: Contract tests for critical paths; CI runs them; but command-to-test coverage ratio < 70%
criteria.l3_definition: Command-to-test coverage ratio >= 70%; coverage matrix auto-generated; CI fails on gap. ALL conditions required.
criteria.l4_definition: L3 + golden-path CLI tests; mutation testing; coverage trend tracking

criteria.l0_definition: No test quality rules or checks exist
criteria.l1_definition: Test quality policy + schema + semgrep rules defined; but not enforced in CI
criteria.l2_definition: Semgrep rules or check_test_quality.py running in CI advisory mode; findings tracked; threshold calibration incomplete
criteria.l3_definition: ALL: (a) CI gate blocking mode, (b) zero bulk-generation markers, (c) shallow_render_ratio < 0.10, (d) assertion_density >= 2.0, (e) threshold calibration documented, (f) managed repo sync verified
criteria.l4_definition: L3 + quality trend dashboard; suppression expiry policy; near-duplicate detection; sampled audit or mutation-level quality verification
